div.head: Mr. Hunter to Sir F. Bruce
div.p: Sir: I have the honor to acknowledge the receipt of your note of yesterday, transmitting to this government and to Mrs. Lincoln copies of resolutions passed at a public meeting of the inhabitants of Southport, England, on the occasion of the lamentable events which have recently occurred in the United States. In reply I have the honor to state that the copy for Mrs. Lincoln will be forwarded to its destination, and on a future occasion the subject will receive further attention.
I have the honor to be, with high consideration, sir, your obedient servant,

postscript.p: Hon. Sir Frederick W. A. Bruce, &c., &c., &c.
